item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely a great base from which to develop more, but that's really all it is right now: bones waiting for flesh. Novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has potential. I can't help but to want to read more about the city, the architecture, the culture, the artefacts, why they would be thaumiel class. So I would be happy if the article is expanded as mentioned in the comment. I think an exploration log would really add a lot to the article and could help to make the city itself more interesting. I also appreciate the detail on the naval crafts in the comment.

I enjoyed the article. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can see this becoming the foundation of a great canon, with each item having its own associated articles (scps, tales, maybe a -J). As it stands I love it, but I'm a sucker for archaeological scps in general.

The one thing I will nitpick is that the article states that almost everything the foundation knows about Aquarian Disco was learned on the expedition, but never expands on that statement at all. You've stated you want further articles in the series to expand on that information, which is a great idea, but I would hope to see at least a few small things in here to justify the inclusion of the aformentioned statement

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this sticks to close to the tone and form of the SCP format, given its subject matter. While I appreciate the fantastic described as though it is mundane as much as the next SCP reader, with a premise like this I expect at least a little color. I spent the whole time I read this waiting for that breakthrough moment that never came.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
First skip I've written in over three years, this one's about the US government finding and looting Atlantis.

To start with, some crit credits. A lot of people glanced at this, so if I forgot your name, let me know:

  • Conwell
  • TyGently
  • Taffeta
  • tawny
  • A Random Day
  • ch00bakka
  • Anorrack
  • minathewitch

Second, some NOTES:

  • The list of missing superweapons here is meant to serve as a potential hook for future articles (skips, tales, goi formats), not unlike the Little Misters list. My hope is to eventually build a much larger and more detailed view of AQUARIAN DISCO and their history by offering snippets in each superweapon article, and in the process also provide a look at some of the political and diplomatic interactions that go on behind the Veil. If you're interested in taking a stab at one of these, hit me up.
  • There's more to the containment here than just the blockade, but the Foundation isn't privy to all of the details of the GOC's activities.
  • SCPS Jump Jacked and Lunatic Leap (as well as Daring Dash) are all clandestine aircraft carriers and submarine tenders — they look like normal civilian ships, but can hold one or two submarines in internal holds, and carry a few wings of aircraft. The GOC vessels are straight-up warships, probably comparable to a light cruiser.
